FR-Systems: General|The system shall provide general functionalities for the integrated library system.,FR
FR-Systems: Consoles and Dashboards|The console interface must be user-friendly, allowing users to easily navigate through different sections of the library management software. The dashboard should display real-time data on book availability, loans, reservations, etc., in a clear and concise manner.,FR
FR-Systems: Business Rules|The system must enforce business rules such as maximum loan duration for books, fines for late returns, overdue limits before renewal is allowed, minimum number of copies required per book category. The software should also support the creation and modification of these rules without manual intervention.,FR
FR-Systems: Data Recovery|The system shall implement a robust data recovery mechanism that ensures all user information, transaction records, inventory details are recoverable in case of any failure or accidental deletion within 24 hours. The process should be transparent and not disrupt the normal operation.,FR
FR-Systems: Security|The software must incorporate strong security measures to protect sensitive data such as personal user information, financial transactions from unauthorized access. It shall include features like multi-factor authentication for users accessing administrative functions, encryption of stored data at rest and in transit, regular audits of system logs.,FR
FR-Systems: Maintenance|The software must be designed with maintainability as a core principle to facilitate easy updates without disrupting the service. It should support modular architecture allowing individual components or modules to be updated independently from others.,FR
FR-Systems: Client Management|The system shall provide functionalities for client management, including registration of new users, updating user profiles with contact information and preferences, managing access levels (librarian, member), etc. The software should ensure data integrity during updates or changes to the profile information.,FR
FR-Systems: Queries and Reports|The system must support complex querying capabilities allowing librarians to generate reports on various aspects such as book circulation statistics, user activity logs, inventory statuses, etc. The reporting feature should be customizable with filters for date ranges, categories, or specific users.,FR
NFR-Systems: General|The system shall have a response time of less than 2 seconds under normal load conditions to ensure efficient operation and good user experience.,NFR
NFR-Systems: Consoles and Dashboards|The console interface must be responsive with no more than 1 second page loading times, even on slower network connections. The dashboard should update data in real time without noticeable delays or interruptions to the display.,NFR
NFR-Systems: Data Recovery|In case of a system failure that results in loss of user information and transaction records, recovery must be completed within 24 hours with no more than 1% error rate. The process should not require manual input from users or administrators to initiate.,NFR
NFR-Systems: Security|The software shall maintain an uptime of at least 99.9%, ensuring that security measures do not impede system availability and performance. All updates related to the security features must be backward compatible with existing data formats.,NFR
NFR-Systems: Maintenance|Maintenance activities should have a negligible impact on user experience, aiming for less than 0.1% downtime during scheduled maintenance windows.,NFR
NFR-Systems: Client Management|The system shall maintain an error rate of less than 0.5% when updating client profiles to ensure data integrity and reliability in the management process.,NFR
NFR-Systems: Queries and Reports|Querying capabilities must not degrade performance by more than 10%, even with complex or large datasets being processed, ensuring that reporting remains a valuable tool for librarians without affecting system responsiveness.,NFR
